title: Vitamin B12 Fact Sheet for Health Professionals
supports_claim: B12 is relevant to neurologic/hematologic function and deficiency-risk contexts; deficiency correction has stronger evidence than generic wellness energy claims.
does_not_support: Does not prove B12 improves peptide efficacy or guarantees energy in nondeficient clients.
evidence_directness: direct for nutrient biology/deficiency; indirect for wellness support

title: Biotin Fact Sheet for Health Professionals
supports_claim: Biotin is involved in carboxylase enzymes and is marketed for hair/skin/nails; evidence for benefit outside deficiency is limited.
does_not_support: Does not prove hair growth or GHK-Cu synergy in nondeficient clients.
evidence_directness: direct for nutrient biology; limited for nondeficient aesthetic outcome

title: Biotin Interference with Troponin Lab Tests
supports_claim: Biotin can interfere with certain lab assays, including troponin.
does_not_support: Does not prohibit all biotin use.

title: Vitamin C Fact Sheet for Health Professionals
supports_claim: Vitamin C is required for collagen biosynthesis and antioxidant function; high intake has renal/oxalate considerations.
does_not_support: Does not prove IV vitamin C improves GHK-Cu outcomes, skin outcomes, or peptide efficacy.
evidence_directness: direct for collagen/nutrient biology; indirect for aesthetic wellness support

title: Magnesium Fact Sheet for Health Professionals
supports_claim: Magnesium is involved in energy production and neuromuscular function; renal impairment increases toxicity risk.
does_not_support: Does not prove magnesium IV improves peptide outcomes.
evidence_directness: direct for nutrient biology/safety; indirect for wellness support

title: GHK peptide as a natural modulator of multiple cellular pathways in skin repair / regeneration literature
supports_claim: GHK-Cu maps to skin repair, extracellular matrix, collagen/glycosaminoglycan, antioxidant and remodeling biology.
does_not_support: Does not prove IV biotin/vitamin C/glutathione enhances GHK-Cu outcomes.
evidence_directness: mechanistic/direct for GHK-Cu skin axis; indirect for IV ingredient support

title: MOTS-c: A promising mitochondrial-derived peptide for therapeutic exploitation
supports_claim: MOTS-c maps to mitochondrial/metabolic/AMPK energy biology.
does_not_support: Does not prove NAD/NR/glutathione IV improves MOTS-c effects.

title: SS-31 mitochondrial-targeting peptide review
supports_claim: SS-31 maps to mitochondrial redox and membrane/cardiolipin biology.
does_not_support: Does not prove NAD/NR/glutathione IV improves SS-31 outcomes.

title: Intravenous infusion of NAD+ versus nicotinamide riboside: retrospective tolerability pilot
supports_claim: Preliminary human IV tolerability data for NAD+ and NR/Niagen IV; NR is distinct from NAD+.
does_not_support: Does not prove durable energy outcomes, anti-aging, disease treatment, or peptide synergy.
evidence_directness: direct for IV NAD/NR tolerability; indirect for wellness energy support

title: Niagen Plus product identity
supports_claim: Niagen is nicotinamide riboside chloride / NR, a NAD+ precursor.
does_not_support: Does not independently prove clinical outcomes.

title: DailyMed Egrifta SV / tesamorelin label
supports_claim: Tesamorelin maps to GH/IGF-1 axis; glucose/IGF-1/retinopathy review matters.
does_not_support: Does not prove IV amino acids, B vitamins or NAD improve GH peptide outcomes.
evidence_directness: direct for peptide axis and safety context

title: MSD/Merck Manual: Intravenous Vitamin Therapy / Myers' Cocktail
supports_claim: Broad IV wellness benefit evidence is limited; safety screening matters.
does_not_support: Does not validate generic peptide-IV efficacy.
evidence_directness: direct for evidence-limit and safety posture
